title: pericallis lanata var. cyanophthalma
intro: Pericallis lanata var. cyanophthalma, commonly known as the blue-flowered pericallis or blue Madeira, is a flowering plant native to the island of Madeira in Portugal. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pericallis lanata var. cyanophthalma include its potential to reduce fever, soothe digestive issues, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its flowers and leaves, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
